meaning: adj.不能赎回的,不能兑现的,不可救药的
examples: There seems to be no one they consider irredeemable.
He is still, in the eyes of some, an irredeemable misogynist.
"I do look forward to it, because I do love playing someone so irredeemable," he says.
" If the court strikes down Obamacare, Rosen argued, Roberts' "stated goal of presiding over a less divisive court will be viewed as an irredeemable failure.
He really believes Earth is headed for irredeemable climatic catastrophe.
